headlines.title: Let debate it : ECB President tells Euronews as Spain pitches joint borrowing plan
Turkey and Iraq Move to Keep Critical Oil Export Route Alive
headlines.summary: Iraq just bought itself another year. Turkey and Iraq are expected to sign a 12-month extension of the agreement governing the Iraq-Turkey crude oil pipeline within days, preventing a July 27 expiration that threatened one of Baghdad's few remaining viable oil export routes. headlines.title: U.S. Oil Dominance Keeps Growing Despite Lower Prices
headlines.summary: While much of the world spent 2025 debating peak oil, the United States was busy setting another production record. headlines.title: Indonesia Receives First Russian Crude Shipment Under April Supply Deal
headlines.summary: Indonesia has recently welcomed the first cargo of Russian crude oil under a deal Southeast Asia’s biggest economy struck with Moscow in April. headlines.title: Shippers Cool on Green Fuels as Costs Stay Too High
headlines.summary: The shipping industry is being blamed by climate activists for emitting a solid portion of the world’s atmospheric carbon dioxide. headlines.title: High Oil Prices Are Fueling a New Heavy Crude Rush in Canada
headlines.summary: Who says price isn't a rationing mechanism? Rather than sink billions into long-term oil sands projects, Alberta energy companies are increasingly chasing the Clearwater formation, a conventional heavy oil field that allows producers to capitalize on rising crude prices far more

headlines.title: EU Eyes 2040 Electrification Target to Slash Oil and Gas Dependence
headlines.summary: The European Commission is set to unveil next week an electrification target for 2040 in a bid to reduce the need for fossil fuels and strengthen the renewable energy sector in the EU, according to a draft proposal seen by Bloomberg News. headlines.title: Ukraine Hits Two Russian Fuel Tankers as Moscow's Fuel Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: Ukraine hit two fuel tankers overnight offshore Russia’s southern coast in an expanding campaign to deepen the fuel crisis in Russia. headlines.title: Pakistan Rushes to Buy Emergency LNG After Qatar Cargo Is Canceled
headlines.summary: Pakistan is forced to turn to the spot LNG market once again after the re-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz has canceled a cargo from Qatar.
